text: Understanding the interactions between targets is crucial for robust Multi-Object Tracking (MOT). In this work, we propose a TransMOT model that utilizes the power of spatial-temporal graph transformers to effectively model the interactions between the targets. To this end, we present a way of representing the target trajectories as a sequence of sparse weighted graphs. We then exploit spatial graph transformer encoders, temporal transformer encoders, and spatial graph transformer decoders to obtain faster and better tracking results than using standard Transformers. In addition, a cascade association framework is proposed to handle the low-confidence detections and long-term occlusions, which are often treated as resource-intensive operations for transformers. When evaluated on MOT15, MOT16, MOT17, and MOT20 datasets, our method TransMOT achieves the state-of-the-art performance on all test splits for various types of scenarios including sparse, medium, and crowded scenes.
explanation: The text is cohesive and uses broad, claim-heavy language with no concrete experimental results or numbers, a pattern commonly seen in AI-generated abstracts but not exclusive to human-written papers.
